codeReview.summary: The commit only adds tests. Search coverage looks reasonable, but three claimed assertions are not actually secured: the scanner screen is still deadlocked on its loading state, the settings regression suite tests the wrong rows, and the offline-first-launch tests stub out the network-sensitive path they are supposed to prove.

issues.description: `requestPermission()` returns early whenever `cameraRef.current` is null, but the `<Camera>` is only rendered after `hasPermission` becomes truthy. That leaves the screen permanently stuck in the `hasPermission === null` loading branch, so the claimed `camera + scan→handleIncomingUrl` regression coverage is blessing a broken flow rather than proving it works.

VAL-UX-053 for this feature was supposed to pin the About/version row and external-link rows, but this new suite explicitly targets different rows (`Lock wallet`, `Biometric unlock`, `Export backup`, `Import backup`, `Agent DID`). The actual Settings screen also only renders those rows, so the required About/external-link surface is neither implemented nor verified.

The offline-first-launch checks mock `@enbox/agent.initialize()` to return a mnemonic immediately, so the tests at the bottom of this file never exercise the real initialization / DWN-registration path under network failure. They would still pass even if first-launch actually failed offline, which leaves VAL-UX-049 unproven.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The current WalletConnect scanner permission flow depends on a `react-native-camera-kit` ref before the `<Camera>` is rendered, which makes both the app flow and Jest coverage fail closed into the loading state. This constraint/bug is not called out anywhere in mission shared state, and the worker handoff treated source-grep tests as a substitute.

summary: Review failed. The feature adds four test files, but three of the claimed assertions remain unverified or contradicted by the code: the scanner flow is still deadlocked before the camera mounts, the settings regression suite does not cover the required About/external-link rows, and the offline-init tests stub out the network-sensitive path they are meant to validate.
